metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State a concrete filename (or a naming convention such as `docs/assets/rebar-quickstart.png`) so the proving command `test -f docs/assets/rebar-quickstart.png` is executable without clarification.
findings.finding: The demo-asset proving command contains `<asset>` as a literal placeholder; no filename convention or default is stated, so the executor must invent the name and the `test -f docs/assets/<asset>` command cannot be executed as written.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Demo asset committed and referenced' AC
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `docs/assets/<asset>` with `docs/assets/rebar-quickstart.png` (or another chosen name) everywhere it appears in the AC and the What section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Fix the badge-row proving command to scope to the first 10 lines, e.g. `head -10 README.md | grep -c 'img.shields.io\|\[!\['` and confirm the count equals 4.
findings.finding: The badge-row proving command searches the entire README but the AC text asserts the constraint applies only to the first 10 lines; the command does not enforce the positional constraint it claims to prove.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Badge row present with exactly 4 badges' AC
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the proving command with: `head -10 README.md | grep -cE '(img\.shields\.io|!\[)' README.md` — more precisely: `count=$(head -10 README.md | grep -cE 'img\.shields\.io'); [ "$count" -eq 4 ]`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Supply concrete shell commands for both prose-count and code-line-count ACs, e.g. `awk '/^```/{exit} {w+=NF} END{print w}' README.md` for words and `awk '/^```/{in_block=!in_block; next} in_block{n++} END{print n}' README.md` for fenced lines, so both checks are deterministic and executable without interpretation.
findings.finding: The '≤15 total code lines' and '≤200 words before first code block' ACs describe the measurement method in prose ('count the fenced block lines', 'word count of the pre-fence region') without providing a concrete, executable proving command; an agent cannot deterministically evaluate either criterion without additional decisions.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Quickstart covers CLI + Python + MCP in ≤15 total code lines' and '≤200 words of prose before the first fenced code block' ACs
findings.suggested_fix: Words AC proving command: `awk '/^```/{exit} {w+=NF} END{if(w>200) exit 1}' README.md`. Code-lines AC: `awk 'BEGIN{n=0} /^```/{f=!f;next} f{n++} END{if(n>15) exit 1}' README.md` (adjust to restrict to the quickstart section if needed).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Convert this documentation requirement into a durable, repo-artifact-verifiable form — e.g. require the rationale to appear in `docs/assets/README.md` or an ADR file, and supply a proving command like `grep -q 'static screenshot' docs/assets/README.md`.
findings.finding: The 'Asset-form decision recorded in the commit message and a ticket comment' AC is not a durable, repo-artifact-verifiable end-state; commit messages cannot be deterministically checked against a criterion from the working tree after merge, and 'ticket comment' is external to the repo.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Asset-form decision (static screenshot; rationale) recorded in the commit message and a ticket comment'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an `docs/assets/DECISIONS.md` (or a comment in README.md itself) that records the asset-form rationale, then prove it with `grep -q 'static screenshot' docs/assets/DECISIONS.md`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the independence-vs-dependency contradiction: either add an explicit merge-gate (this story ships only after onboarding story lands) or replace the hard link with a `<!-- TODO: link once onboarding story ships -->` placeholder that is AC-verified to become live before this story closes.
findings.finding: The plan declares itself 'independent actor, independently shippable' (Why) but the What section introduces a runtime link dependency: 'Contributor pointer near the bottom links CONTRIBUTING.md and docs/your-first-change.md (this story depends_on the onboarding story so the link is never dangling).' If the onboarding story has not yet shipped, merging this README change produces a dangling link — directly contradicting the independently-shippable claim.
findings.location: Why section ('independent actor, independently shippable') vs. What section final bullet ('this story depends_on the onboarding story')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Dependencies / merge order' subsection to Scope stating: 'This story must not be merged to main before docs/your-first-change.md exists; the onboarding story is a prerequisite.' Then remove the 'independently shippable' language, or scope the contributor pointer as a TODO placeholder that becomes a real link only after the prerequisite is satisfied.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Align the badge-row AC verify boundary with the What-section description: change 'first 10 lines' to 'first 15 lines' (or whichever boundary the author intends as authoritative) so the criterion and the design spec name the same constraint.
findings.finding: The badge-row AC verify step checks 'the first 10 lines' but the What section places the badge row 'within ~15 lines' (consistent with the surveyed exemplars). These two specifications name different line boundaries for the same entity, creating ambiguity about where the AC actually tests.
findings.location: AC line 1 ('grep -c … in the first 10 lines = 4') vs. What section item 1 ('H1/logo → one badge row … within ~15 lines')
findings.suggested_fix: Update AC 1 to: 'prove: head -15 README.md | grep -c "img.shields.io\|badge" = 4'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add covering ACs for the three uncovered deliverables: (a) verify brew one-liner is present in the Install section, (b) verify the highlight-bullets block contains between 5 and 8 items and includes 'MCP' and 'Gerrit' (or equivalent keywords), and (c) verify `grep -c 'your-first-change\|CONTRIBUTING' README.md` ≥ 1 in the bottom half of the file.
findings.finding: Three described deliverables have no covering acceptance criterion: (a) the brew install one-liner, (b) the 5–8 highlight bullets (count and required content — tri-surface bullet and two-gate Gerrit dogfooding bullet), and (c) the contributor pointer to CONTRIBUTING.md and docs/your-first-change.md.
findings.location: What section (items 4, 5, and final bullet) vs. Acceptance Criteria section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an alt-text requirement to AC 3: the Markdown image reference must include a descriptive alt string (e.g., `![Terminal session showing rebar ready, rebar claim, and rebar transition closed commands](docs/assets/...)`) covering the content shown in the screenshot, satisfying WCAG 2.1 SC 1.1.1.
findings.finding: The plan commits a terminal screenshot (new user-facing image) but does not specify alt-text for that image, which is required for WCAG 2.1 AA compliance (Success Criterion 1.1.1 Non-text Content). The plan's accessibility rationale ('no motion-accessibility concerns') addresses only reduced motion, not screen-reader accessibility of the image itself.
findings.location: What section item 3 ('Demo asset … static terminal screenshot'); Acceptance Criteria item 3 ('test -f docs/assets/<asset> and README references it')
findings.suggested_fix: Amend AC 3 verify step to: 'README references the asset within the first 40 lines AND the Markdown img tag includes non-empty alt text describing the terminal session content.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC item (e.g., `test -f docs/your-first-change.md`) or a merge-gate note that enforces the depends_on sequencing so the contributor link is verifiably non-dangling before this story ships.
findings.finding: The README will include a link to `docs/your-first-change.md` (from the onboarding story), which the plan asserts will 'never be dangling' because of a depends_on relationship — but no AC or merge-sequencing mechanism enforces this guarantee. If this story ships before the onboarding story, the link will 404.
findings.location: ## What — Contributor pointer bullet; ## Acceptance Criteria (no corresponding AC item)
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC line: `- [ ] Contributor link is non-dangling — prove: \`test -f docs/your-first-change.md\` passes in CI (or merge is explicitly blocked until the onboarding story lands).`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that verifies `docs/your-first-change.md` exists (or, if the onboarding story is a hard prerequisite, cite its ticket ID and state this story must not land before it); alternatively, omit the link from this story's deliverable and add it only in the onboarding story.
findings.finding: docs/your-first-change.md does not exist in the repository, but the plan asserts the contributor-pointer link 'is never dangling' because 'this story depends_on the onboarding story'. No ticket ID is cited for the onboarding story and no AC in this plan verifies the file exists before the link is added. If this story ships before the onboarding story, the README will contain a dead link to docs/your-first-change.md.
findings.location: Scope / Contributor pointer section: 'Contributor pointer near the bottom links CONTRIBUTING.md and docs/your-first-change.md (this story depends_on the onboarding story so the link is never dangling)'
findings.suggested_fix: Either: (a) add an AC: `test -f docs/your-first-change.md` and declare it a hard prerequisite with a named ticket dependency; or (b) replace the dangling link in this story's contributor-pointer with a placeholder that points to CONTRIBUTING.md alone, and let the onboarding story add the docs/your-first-change.md link when that file exists.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that verifies `docs/your-first-change.md` exists (e.g. `test -f docs/your-first-change.md`) before the contributor-pointer link to it is considered shippable, OR change the link to point to `CONTRIBUTING.md` (which already exists) as the fallback until the onboarding story lands.
findings.finding: docs/your-first-change.md does not exist in the repository, yet the plan specifies adding a link to it in the README's contributor pointer section with no AC that enforces the file exists before the link is live.
findings.location: What section, last bullet: 'Contributor pointer near the bottom links CONTRIBUTING.md and `docs/your-first-change.md` (this story depends_on the onboarding story so the link is never dangling).'
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) add a gating AC: `test -f docs/your-first-change.md` — prove the file exists before closing this story; or (b) make the contributor pointer link only to `CONTRIBUTING.md` now and add a follow-up task in the onboarding story to retarget it to `docs/your-first-change.md` once that file is created.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether the badge row edit is a preservation/reorder task or an addition — the current README.md already has exactly 4 badges matching the spec; update the plan to note 'verify existing badge row matches spec' rather than 'add badge row' to avoid duplication.
findings.finding: The plan directs adding a badge row as a new action item, but the current README.md already contains exactly 4 badges (lines 3–6: PyPI version, Python versions, License, CI) — the edit target for this hunk is already in the desired end-state.
findings.location: What section, item 1: '`# rebar` → one badge row, exactly 4 badges: PyPI version, Python versions, CI status, license.' / AC: 'Badge row present with exactly 4 badges'
findings.suggested_fix: Change the plan's item 1 to: 'Verify the existing badge row (lines 3–6) matches the 4-badge spec (PyPI version, Python versions, CI status, license) — no change needed if already correct.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Fix AC1 proof command to use ERE: change `grep -c "img.shields.io\|badge"` to `grep -cE "img.shields.io|badge"` (or equivalent two-pass form) so the badge-count assertion is actually executable.
findings.finding: The AC1 proof command `grep -c "img.shields.io\|badge" README.md` uses POSIX BRE syntax where `\|` is a literal two-character sequence, not alternation. Running this command verbatim returns 0 (no lines contain the literal string `img.shields.io|badge`), not 4. The command needs `-E` for ERE alternation (`grep -cE`), or the pattern should be rewritten as two separate greps piped together. An implementer running the AC exactly as written will get a false-negative and incorrectly conclude the badge row is missing.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1 badge row proof command
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the AC1 proof with: `grep -cE 'img[.]shields[.]io|badge' <(head -10 README.md)` = 4, or equivalently `head -10 README.md | grep -cE 'img\.shields\.io|badge\.svg'`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (e.g. `test -f docs/your-first-change.md`) to this plan so the README's contributor-pointer link is only merged when the target file provably exists, removing the depends_on ordering assumption as the sole guard against a live 404.
findings.finding: The plan's only guard against a dangling contributor link to `docs/your-first-change.md` is the informal assertion 'this story depends_on the onboarding story so the link is never dangling.' No AC in this plan verifies that `docs/your-first-change.md` actually exists before the README is merged. Multiple prior plan-review cache entries confirm the file does not currently exist in the repository. If the onboarding story ships late, is de-scoped, or lands after this story, every user who reads the new README's contributor pointer hits a 404. The depends_on relationship is a social/scheduling guarantee, not a file-system guarantee.
findings.location: What section — 'Contributor pointer near the bottom'; Scope section; implicit ordering assumption
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Acceptance Criteria: `- [ ] Contributor link target exists — prove: `test -f docs/your-first-change.md` exits 0 at merge time (enforce via CI or merge-gate check).`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify and record the exact, currently-published PyPI package name for rebar (run `pip index versions nava-rebar` or check PyPI directly) and confirm it matches the install command and badge URLs before committing.
findings.finding: The install command `pipx install nava-rebar` is an unverified capability assertion: the plan does not confirm that the package is published (or will be published) under exactly that name on PyPI. If the canonical PyPI name differs (e.g. `rebar`, `nava_rebar`, or not yet published), both the install instruction and the two PyPI-dependent badges (version, Python versions) will silently break for every evaluator who reads the README.
findings.location: ## What — Install section; AC badge row (first 10 lines)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a verification step to the acceptance criteria: `pip index versions nava-rebar` must return a non-empty version list, or replace the name with the confirmed canonical name throughout.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether a Homebrew formula/tap for rebar exists; if yes, record the exact `brew install` or `brew tap` + `brew install` command in the plan; if no, remove the brew path from scope or add creating the formula as an explicit dependency.
findings.finding: The `brew one-liner` is mentioned as an install path but is unspecified and unverified: no formula name, tap, or repository is given. It is unclear whether a Homebrew formula for rebar exists at all. If it does not exist, the README will advertise a broken install path to macOS evaluators.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the exact MCP registration command (entry-point name, config key, invocation syntax) before writing the quickstart, and record it in the plan or a linked reference so the implementer is not guessing.
findings.finding: The plan promises a 'one-line MCP registration' in the quickstart but provides no concrete example of what that line is. If the MCP server packaging, entry-point name, or configuration format is not yet finalised, the quickstart cannot be written to the ≤15-line budget and will either be omitted or wrong at implementation time.
findings.location: ## What — Quickstart section; AC: 'Quickstart covers CLI + Python + MCP in ≤15 total code lines'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
